channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
The light in this alley has been broken for a few weeks now

item.title: Abandoned Vehicle, 8th April
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 07/04/26 16:13 Abandoned Vehicle Lat/Long - 51.4126535, -0.0689227 what3words - create.drape.orders Nearest address - 6 Rowan Terrace, Sycamore Grove, Penge, London, SE20 8BB The vehicle was there a few weeks. A flat tire and a damaged front bumper. Reference - 5470497 Map link - open Google map Report history - Status: Received 07/04/2026 16:13 The vehicle was there a few weeks. A flat tire and a damaged front bumper.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rowan Terrace, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Tree support has snapped off, 8th April
item.description:
The pole supporting the tree in the street outside 47 Ainsworth Road E9 7JE has snapped off (it looks as if it has rotted). The protective cage which it was holding up has therefore also fallen off and both are lying on the pavement next to the tree.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ainsworth Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hillsboro Road - Blocked pavement, 8th April
item.description:
Safe route to school, overgrown vegetation between 1 & 3 Hillsboro Road. Safe route to school

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Hillsboro Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damage to street art - little boy figure on ground, 8th April
item.description:
I think this occurred in the 24 period from 0700 on 7 April as i passed yesterday and today (8 April) at the same time and would have noticed yesterday. Is there CCTV you can check. Little boy’s face has been missing for some time. Vandalism?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whitmore Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged Manhole cover, 8th April
item.description:
Damaged has been caused to our bus due the man hole cover been damaged or fallen through.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 96-120, Old Kent Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can tree pit be moved slightly when doing other work, 8th April
item.description:
Outside 34 Groveland Road, Beckenham there is an empty tree pit. It is on the slope between normal level pavement and a lowered curb which is part of access to house front drive. The tree pit has recently had a wooden stake put in which, according to my Tree Friends manual, is indicative of intention to plant a tree in the pit. Closeby some white marks have been placed around a piece of sunken tarmac that forms part of the normal pavement. Presumably indicative of an intention to repair this part of the pavement soon. Can I suggest that LBB used the opportunity of these two pieces of work to move the tree pit slightly, so that it is moved from the slope to a level area of pavement. This would make the area a lot neater and reduce the risk of the new tree being damaged by vehicles crossing the pavement. Please see picture for a better idea of how this suggestion would work in practice. Many thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Groveland Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near 57 Highgate, West Hill, 8th April
item.description:
The alley way is over grown with weeds and has a junction cabinet with the door removed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pond Square, Highgate, Greater London

item.title: Hallway, 8th April
item.description:
Beople in door 21 they always put the bin waste in the hallway it make the all area smell they can keep the waste in the for 2 days please speak to them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing rubbish bins - literring streets with food and waste, 8th April
item.description:
The previous report was marked as fixed but the bins are still overflowing onto the street. The bins on this property on delafield road( waste and recycling) have not been collected. The waste is continually overflowing onto the street and blowing down the road. This has been ongoing since 03.04.26 - causing foxes and rodents to tear open waste - causing further waste issues on the street - waste is food and Sanitary items on the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Delafield Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On pathway lots of rubbish bags, 8th April
item.description:
This is a regular thing same old rubbish being left in same place on the pathway dumped people going around it tripping and all scattered a bout .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marmadon Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
